question: Are there two distinct computer monitors positioned side by side on a desk?
Are oscilloscope probes, jumper wires, and circuit boards visible on the desk surface?
Is the C code (starting with #include) displayed specifically on the Left monitor?
Is the Makefile content (starting with CC=gcc) displayed specifically on the Right monitor?
On the left screen, are the include headers rendered correctly: "#include <stdint.h>" and "#include <stdbool.h>"?
On the left screen, is the 'Ring' struct defined correctly, including "uint8_t data[64]", "uint8_t r", and "uint8_t w"?
On the left screen, is the 'ring_push' function visible with the logic "if (nw == rb->r) return false;"?
On the left screen, is the 'Led' struct defined correctly with fields "ms", "last", and "state"?
On the left screen, is the 'led_step' function rendered with the specific timing check "if (now - l->last < 250)"?
On the left screen, does the 'crc16' function contain the correct hexadecimal constants "0xFFFF" and "0x1021"?
On the left screen, is the 'main' function visible and does it contain an infinite loop "for (;;)"?
On the right screen, are the variable assignments "CC=gcc" and "TARGET=firmware" rendered correctly?
On the right screen, is the CFLAGS line rendered exactly as "CFLAGS=-std=c11 -O2 -Wall -Wextra -Werror"?
On the right screen, are the build rules "$(TARGET): main.o" and "main.o: main.c" rendered correctly?
On the right screen, is the clean rule rendered with the command "rm -f $(TARGET) *.o"?
On the right screen, is the phony target declared as ".PHONY: all clean"?
Do the screens exhibit slight reflections and perspective distortion as requested, without rendering the text illegible?
Are code syntax characters (curly braces {}, semicolons ;, asterisks *, hex prefixes 0x) rendered with distinct glyph integrity (not merged, blurry, or hallucinated)?
Is the text sufficiently sharp and legible on both monitors despite the perspective and simulated screen texture?
Does the image appear as a realistic photo-style rendering rather than a vector illustration or cartoon?
